skill.name: angular-migration
skill.description: Guide AngularJS to Angular migrations with hybrid app setup, component rewrites, dependency injection updates, routing changes, and forms migration patterns.
skill.summary: Plan and execute AngularJS to Angular migrations with hybrid upgrade patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown examples for Angular migration rather than executable skill behavior. The HTTP examples use relative application API paths, and no prompt injection, exfiltration, malicious command execution, or hidden business logic was found in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged locations are fenced Markdown code blocks and Angular template literals that document migration examples. They are not Ruby or shell backtick execution and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations appear inside documentation examples in SKILL.md, including TypeScript, JavaScript, HTML, and plain text fences. No runnable script wrapper or instruction executes these snippets automatically.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative HTTP Examples Flagged as Network Access
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The HTTP calls are AngularJS and Angular service examples using relative paths such as application API routes. They do not contact third-party endpoints or transmit secrets from the skill environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both network indicators are sample application code in documentation, not active skill code. The paths are relative API examples, with no external host, token handling, or exfiltration pattern.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Indicator
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged description line explains Angular migration scope and contains no cryptographic operation, algorithm choice, key material, or hash usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is frontmatter text about framework migration. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Indicators
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged lines describe migration approaches, setup phases, and resource names. They do not collect host data, enumerate files, inspect processes, or run reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are documentation headings, migration steps, and resource references. No evidence found of system discovery commands or environment inspection logic.

content.value_statement: Legacy AngularJS applications are hard to modernize without disrupting delivery. This skill provides structured migration guidance for hybrid apps, services, components, routing, and forms.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use big bang, incremental, and vertical slice migration strategies.
Shows hybrid AngularJS and Angular bootstrapping with UpgradeModule.
Demonstrates converting controllers, directives, services, routes, and forms.
Covers dependency injection patterns for upgrading and downgrading services.
Provides a phased migration timeline for planning delivery work.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include runnable migration automation.
It uses simplified examples that require adaptation for production applications.
It does not assess a repository or produce a complete migration estimate by itself.
Referenced support files are named in the guide but are not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Assess a Legacy AngularJS Application
use_cases.description: Choose a migration strategy, identify early service work, and plan an incremental delivery path.

use_cases.title: Convert AngularJS Features
use_cases.description: Translate controllers, directives, services, forms, and routes into modern Angular patterns.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Migration Roadmap
use_cases.description: Break migration work into phases with infrastructure, feature conversion, cleanup, and testing milestones.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Migration Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my AngularJS application context and recommend big bang, incremental, or vertical slice migration. Explain the tradeoffs and first steps.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Hybrid Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a hybrid AngularJS and Angular setup plan using ngUpgrade. Include bootstrapping, module boundaries, testing, and rollout order.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Feature Area
prompt_templates.prompt: Help migrate this AngularJS feature to Angular. Cover controller to component changes, service updates, routing changes, and form handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Migration Roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this AngularJS to Angular migration roadmap for risk, sequencing errors, dependency injection issues, routing gaps, and missing tests.

output_examples.input: A team needs to migrate a large AngularJS application without stopping feature delivery.
output: Recommended approach: incremental hybrid migration with ngUpgrade.
Start with shared services and build infrastructure before user interface rewrites.
Migrate one feature slice at a time and test each boundary before cleanup.

output_examples.input: A developer needs to convert an AngularJS directive into Angular.
output: Map isolate scope bindings to Angular inputs and outputs.
Move template behavior into an Angular component class.
Replace AngularJS event callbacks with EventEmitter outputs.

output_examples.input: A migration plan mixes AngularJS services with Angular components.
output: Use upgrade and downgrade patterns deliberately at framework boundaries.
Keep dependency injection tokens explicit for legacy services.
Document each temporary bridge so it can be removed during cleanup.

best_practices: Migrate shared services and infrastructure before broad user interface rewrites.
Use hybrid mode for large applications that need continuous delivery.
Test each migrated feature at the framework boundary before moving to the next slice.
anti_patterns: Rewriting the full application without a delivery or rollback plan.
Mixing AngularJS and Angular patterns inside the same feature without clear ownership.
Removing ngUpgrade before all legacy dependencies and routes are replaced.

faq.question: Does this skill run migration scripts?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples for planning and implementing an AngularJS to Angular migration.

faq.question: Can it help with hybrid applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains ngUpgrade patterns for running AngularJS and Angular side by side.

faq.question: Does it cover dependency injection changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for making Angular services available to AngularJS and legacy services available to Angular.

faq.question: Is it useful for small applications?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help compare a complete rewrite with incremental migration options.

faq.question: Does it include routing migration guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows the concept of moving AngularJS routes to Angular Router definitions.

faq.question: What should teams prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Teams should collect application structure, service dependencies, route ownership, test coverage, and release constraints.
